Fix toolbar About button not appearing

commit.message:
Add early chunk-reload guard to fix stale-deploy loading hang

GitHub Pages sends Cache-Control: max-age=600 on index.html, so after
a deploy the browser may serve cached HTML referencing hashed chunks
that no longer exist. Since main.tsx never boots in this case, the
previous error handlers in main.tsx couldn't fire.

- Add public/chunk-reload-guard.js loaded as a classic script before
  the module bundle — catches chunk load failures and reloads with a
  cache-busting query param to force a fresh HTML fetch
- Remove duplicate chunk-reload logic from main.tsx (now redundant)
- Exclude chunk-reload-guard.js from SW precache (must be fetched fresh)
- Update meta description and OG/Twitter tags to "Local-first" copy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Decimal detection: check isinstance instead of pd.to_numeric

pd.to_numeric() cannot convert Python Decimal objects — it raises
TypeError which was silently caught. Now directly checks if the
first non-null value is a Decimal instance and converts with
astype(float), which handles Decimal correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add historical section: why hadronic SUSY was abandoned in the 1970s

Traces the path from Miyazawa (1966) through QCD (1973), SUGRA (1976),
and strings (1984) to explain why SUSY migrated to the Planck scale.
The missing tools — Seiberg duality (1994), ISS (2006), Koide extensions
— were developed for TeV-scale physics but are exactly what makes the
sBootstrap possible at f_pi = 92 MeV. The historical detour through
high-energy SUSY was a necessary one: the tools built along the way
are what closes the circle back to Miyazawa's hadronic superalgebra.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: upgrade @testing-library/react to support React 19

AWS Amplify deployment was failing due to React version conflict:
- Project uses React 19.2.3
- @testing-library/react@14.1.2 only supports React ^18.0.0

Solution: Upgrade to @testing-library/react@15.0.7 which supports React 19.x

This fixes the npm dependency resolution error during build:
'peer react@"^18.0.0" from @testing-library/react@14.3.1'

fix: pass custom-plugins config in local/download-files mode (#13149)

* fix: pass custom-plugins through in download-files/local mode for Java SDK

The `custom-plugins` config option was silently dropped when running
`fern generate --local` because `JavaSdkDownloadFilesCustomConfig` was
missing the `customPlugins` field, and the manual config reconstruction
in `runInDownloadFilesModeHook` omitted it from the builder.

Add GitLab pipeline tracking, objectives UI, share links, and pipeline dashboard

- Sync GitLab pipelines and jobs with retry inference (API doesn't set retried flag)
- Pipeline metrics API: weekly stats, slowest jobs, flaky jobs endpoints
- Pipeline dashboard with duration trend chart, stats cards, slowest/flaky tables
- Objectives CRUD with evidence linking and KR auto-update from data sources
- Share link support with read-only JWT tokens (24h expiry)
- Auth middleware split into owner vs share token verification
- Extract paginateGitLab into shared sync util

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix STA sync-context deadlock in Shutdown() and Dispose() (#17)

* Fix STA sync-context deadlock in Shutdown() and Dispose()

Add ConfigureAwait(false) to all await sites in the library to prevent
deadlocks when sync wrappers (Shutdown, Dispose) are called from a
single-threaded SynchronizationContext (Avalonia, WPF, WinForms).

Add regression test using a minimal single-threaded SynchronizationContext
that reproduces the deadlock without any UI framework dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions to stable 10.0.3

Replaces prerelease 10.0.0-preview.1.25080.5 with stable 10.0.3,
fixing NU5104 warning about prerelease dependency in a stable package.

dashboard, timeline, agent, integrations, security, conclusion) into

chat examples, integrations, and references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 544p resolution, rework HuMo quality presets

New resolution: 544p (960×544) — 2x upscale gives clean 1080p.

Preset changes:
- PREVIEW: fp8 + LoRA + 384p + 6 steps + CFG=1 (~48s/clip)
- DRAFT: fp8 + 480p + 15 steps (dual CFG)
- FAST: alias for PREVIEW
- FINAL: fp8 + 544p + 30 steps (~26-28GB VRAM estimated)

Default config now targets 544p/30 steps instead of 720p/50 steps,
since upscaling handles the final resolution bump.

feat: badge "Falta creativo" en Ad Sets Manager para recs aprobadas

Cuando se aprueba una recomendacion creative_refresh, la rec desaparece
de la pestana Recomendaciones. Pero en el Ad Sets Manager no habia
ninguna indicacion de que ese ad set tiene una accion pendiente.

Ahora aparece un badge amarillo pulsante "Falta creativo" junto al
nombre del ad set cuando tiene una rec creative_refresh aprobada
cuya accion aun no se ha ejecutado (action_executed != true).

- Usa datos existentes de trackingRecs (no requiere API extra)
- Se muestra como primer badge (mas prominente que tracking/recs/insights)
- Desaparece automaticamente cuando se sube el creativo (auto-link
  marca action_executed=true)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Add intent classification and pre-generated answer pool

Intent classification:
- classifyIntent() tags voice input (hint_request, guess, skip, question, etc.)
- Annotations help Claude interpret messy voice transcripts correctly
- "yes" after hint offer is tagged as 'response' not 'guess'

Answer pool pre-generation:
- New /api/generate-pool endpoint batch-generates 10 validated answers
- Uses tool_choice to force structured output with letter validation
- Pool fetched in background when category/difficulty are set
- drawFromPool() provides instant round starts — no waiting for Claude
- Auto-refills when pool drops to 2 or fewer entries
- Falls back to Claude's create_round tool if pool is empty
- Cache version bumped to v53

Initial commit: BEDR Mobile App (React Native / Expo SDK 52)

Full React Native mobile application for the BEDR EMS reservation system.

Features:
- Authentication (email/password, Google OAuth, Meta OAuth)
- Dashboard with upcoming lessons, active packages, badges
- 3-step booking wizard (package → calendar → confirm)
- Profile with 7 tabs (info, purchases, lessons, badges, offers, referral, GDPR)
- Push notifications via Expo Push API
- Deep linking (bedr:// scheme)
- Czech localization with vocative case greetings
- Error boundary, pull-to-refresh, safe areas

Tech stack:
- Expo SDK 52, TypeScript, React Navigation v7
- Axios with JWT interceptor + expo-secure-store
- expo-notifications for push notifications
- date-fns with Czech locale
